title: Federation provenance stamp
description: The source-of-record stamp a federated read emits — the canonical shape Prawf logs and a Craffter audits (awen-weave craidd/federation.py::provenance_stamp, federation spec §4, v0.1-schema.md §10 item 8). Operationalises SOV-001 federated sovereignty and ADJ-RETURN-001: reference, don't copy. The claim-level fail-loud gate is only the two qualifiers federated_from + source_ran_at plus binding=federated (SCH-CLAIM-001 $defs.qualifiers); THIS is the full stamp that accompanies them. Fail-loud (invariants 2 & 3): source.instance, source.ran_at_utc and source.paths are all required.

binding.description: A stamp is only emitted for federated reads.

source_of_record.description: Identity of the instance a datum is federated FROM (SourceOfRecord).

paths.description: Logical name -> path relative to root; the read-only source outputs referenced (invariant 1). Non-empty (invariant 2).

ran_at_utc.description: The SOURCE's own run-UTC, read from its stats/manifest — never re-derived by the consumer (invariant 3, verify-not-recall). Required.

consumer.description: The consuming instance and the graded link it created (invariant 4, grade-and-link).

craidd_node.description: Target node on the consumer's Craidd.

craidd_source.description: Source id in the consumer's Craidd.

grade.description: A | B | C — the graded link.

read.description: The consumer's read metadata for this federated datum.

federated_utc.description: The consumer's read UTC (NOT the source run-UTC).
